Clarified the chance-coincidence (P_cc) language & interpretation in the manuscript. Added a Chance-coincidence probability subsubsection to sections/toa.tex: states plainly what the test asks (unrelated CHIME burst mimicking match in time AND position AND DM), the Poisson estimator mu_i = R*Omega*2dt*f_DM with each factor named, rare-event reading P_cc ~ mu, conservative chance-maximizing inputs (Catalog-1 rate cited CHIME2021Catalog), and that real per-event tolerances only lower it. Residuals para now gives concrete numbers (per-burst ~1e-9, sample Sum_mu ~5e-8) sourced from pipeline experiment-chance-coincidence-falsealarm.md. Reworded results.tex/conclusions.tex 'baseline window' -> 'conservative, chance-maximizing windows'. latexmk clean, all xrefs+citation resolve. Description/interpretation only; does NOT re-derive arithmetic from raw inputs (that is the V6 certification work).
Verified Cloudflare MCP config, bearer token presence, direct MCP docs call, and fresh Codex docs tool call; failure is stale current-session exposure only.

Fixed Galactic-model inconsistency in the DM budget (Table 3). DM_MW column was NE2001 (pygedm in sightline_budget.py) but the manuscript declares NE2025 (Ocker2026, integrated to 30 kpc) as the MW default in observations sec:obs-mw. Regenerated DM_MW for all twelve sightlines with NE2025 via mwprop.nemod.NE2025.ne2025 (ndir=-1, dmd=30, classic=False) in flits env; NE2025 runs ~4-9 pc/cm3 lower than NE2001 except 20240122A (-38, NE2001 overpredicts at b=10.4deg). Propagated into DM_host = DM_obs - DM_MW - DM_cos - DM_int for the 9 z-known sightlines (arithmetic self-consistent, assert-checked). Both negative-residual sightlines stay negative (-57,-55) so narrative holds. Updated budget_table.tex values + caption (NE2001->NE2025 cite Ocker2026), sections/budget.tex prose (NE2025 adopted, NE2001/YMW16 kept as cross-checks), results.tex host outlier 163->167. latexmk clean, Ocker2026 resolves. Scratch: ne2025_budget_dmmw.py + propagate_dmhost.py.

Correction: the DM_MW NE2001->NE2025 regeneration above (mislabeled P4·V4) belongs to lane P4·V5 (DM-budget re-validation). Note this touches the same surface V5 will formally re-certify (its oracle list includes NE2001/YMW16 pass-through and regenerating the budget table from the verified census); my edit fixes the model-choice inconsistency in the current hand-typed table but does NOT close V5 - the emitter-generated table + oracle tests remain the V5 deliverable.
